rationale: FOMC Minutes (hawkish, some policymakers sought June hike) combined with Warsh's first congressional testimony on July 14-15 and incoming June CPI data are fresh rate-hike-reinforcing catalysts that bear directly on silver via real yields and USD. The US-Iran Strait of Hormuz escalation is transmitting into silver-negative oil inflation expectations, not a safe-haven bid. Technically, silver has confirmed a Break of Structure below the ascending channel with 'Strong Sell' readings across hourly-to-weekly timeframes, and the $57.71 intraday low is the immediate downside gatekeeper before $56.61
key_drivers: hawkish FOMC Minutes + potential Fed rate hike priced for 2026
Fed Chair Warsh congressional testimony (July 14-15) risk event
US-Iran Strait of Hormuz escalation → oil spike → inflation/rate-hike expectations
confirmed technical Break of Structure + death-cross risk on daily chart
June CPI release (binary: hot print accelerates selloff, soft print = main reversal risk)
NY vault refill bearish; Shanghai tightness limits but does not arrest downside
